taxonomy: Chelyella tubercularis belongs to the phylum Arthropoda, specifically within the class Maxillopoda and the order Harpacticoida, and further classified as a species of the genus Chelyella. This classification is based on its physical characteristics and evolutionary relationships with other organisms. Chelyella tubercularis is a type of copepod, a small crustacean found in aquatic environments. Within the order Harpacticoida, Chelyella tubercularis is one of the many species that exhibit unique adaptations for survival in their ecosystems. As a member of the phylum Arthropoda, Chelyella tubercularis shares characteristics with other arthropods, including jointed limbs and a hard exoskeleton.
